best_hook_patterns: Identity: 'You didn't open a shop to become an admin.'
Result-first: '47 bookings. $3,240 revenue. Zero phone calls.'
Specific day: 'Your Monday chair is empty. Here's why.'

top_post_examples.hook: Real barber, real story — no-shows cost him $800/month
top_post_examples.why: Real barber voice, specific pain point (no-shows), peer-to-peer tone

top_post_examples.hook: How many no-shows did you get this week?
top_post_examples.why: Direct question format, weekend audience is present and scrolling

top_post_examples.hook: Your Monday chair is empty. Here's why.
top_post_examples.why: Education drives saves + reach simultaneously — dual win format

zero_post_patterns.hook: Start your free trial today at noblelink.app
zero_post_patterns.why: Barbers mentally off on Sundays, promotional intent misread the moment

key_insights: Reels drive reach (643–856), Feed posts drive engagement rate (57.7% avg ER)
Social Proof is Noble's sharpest weapon — 59% ER proves content-market fit
Education Reels are the only format that wins both reach AND saves simultaneously
DM shares are the top algorithmic signal — add 'Send this to a barber who needs it' to every Reel
Sunday ENGAGE (not CONVERT) — 748 reach wasted on 25.94% ER when promotional
5.5% save rate across week (223 saves / 4024 reach) — well above 1-2% industry benchmark
Follow CTAs are missing — reach is strong but zero follower conversion; add explicit follow prompt to 3+ posts
strategist_note: W23 had 4,024 total reach and 223 saves with 0 new followers — the audience saves but doesn't follow yet. Priority for W24: (1) carousel Tuesday for saves + shares, (2) DM share prompt on every Reel, (3) explicit follow CTA in at least 3 posts, (4) replace Sunday promo with community poll.
